message:
fix(dtype,io,ndarray,random): add clippy allows for intentional lossy casts, harden npz offset and stride conversions, and fix logfactorial index safety

dtype: Adds #[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation, clippy::cast_sign_loss)]
to set_f64 and set_i128 — these methods intentionally perform lossy casts
(e.g. f64 → u8) as part of NumPy-compatible type coercion semantics. The
truncation is by design, not a bug.

io: write_npz_bytes_with_compression now uses u32::try_from(buf.len()) for
the local file header offset instead of bare `as u32`, returning
NpzArchiveContractViolation when the archive exceeds 4GB (ZIP32 limit).

ndarray: is_c_contiguous and is_f_contiguous replace bare `self.item_size
as isize` with isize::try_from().unwrap_or(isize::MAX), preventing silent
truncation on platforms where item_size exceeds isize::MAX.

random: logfactorial replaces `k < LOGFACT.len() as i64` comparison +
`LOGFACT[k as usize]` indexing with usize::try_from(k) + bounds check,
eliminating the possibility of negative-to-unsigned wrap on debug_assert-
disabled builds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
